address: 83/159 Phetkasem Road, Nong Kae, Hua Hin, Prachuap Khiri Khan 77110
best_time_to_visit: Friday evening for the full experience with noticeably smaller crowds than Saturday

description.en:
## Hua Hin's Creative Weekend Market

Cicada Market is an upscale art and creative market on Phetkasem Road that has become one of Hua Hin's signature weekend attractions. Unlike typical Thai night markets, Cicada focuses on handmade goods, original artwork, and artisan food — giving it a distinctly bohemian atmosphere that appeals to both Thai and international visitors.

## Art and Shopping

Local artists display and sell original paintings, sketches, and photography — many depicting Hua Hin's coastal landscapes and Thai rural scenes. Beyond art, you will find stalls selling handmade jewellery, leather goods, fashionable beachwear, ceramics, and unique souvenirs you will not find at regular tourist markets. You can even have your portrait sketched on the spot for a few hundred baht.

## Food and Drinks

The food section is a highlight in its own right, offering an international spread that goes well beyond standard Thai street food. Italian wood-fired pizza stalls sit next to American BBQ pork rib vendors, Japanese sushi counters, and traditional Thai dessert makers. Most dishes cost between 80 and 200 THB. Cocktail bars and craft beer stalls add to the relaxed evening vibe.

## Live Entertainment

A central stage hosts live music ranging from jazz ensembles to acoustic Thai pop acts. On some evenings you will also catch puppet shows for children and contemporary dance performances. The combination of music, art, and good food creates a festival-like atmosphere that is perfect for a leisurely evening stroll.

## Getting There

Cicada Market is on Phetkasem Road near Soi Hua Hin 83, about a 10-minute drive south of the town centre. Taxis and songthaew can drop you right at the entrance.
description.nl: Weekend kunst- en creatieve markt met handgemaakte producten, livemuziek en ambachtelijke eetkramen

tips: Go on Friday to enjoy the same vendors and entertainment with much smaller crowds than Saturday
Bring cash — most art vendors and small food stalls do not accept credit cards
Allow at least two hours to properly browse the art, eat, and enjoy a live performance
